title: Crispy Sweet Polish Potatoes Tart
description: A sweet, airy tart featuring caramelized potatoes in a low‑sodium, tandoor‑baked crust, finished with a light cream drizzle—perfect for a hangover cure.

ingredients.name: Low‑sodium cream
ingredients.notes: use light or reduced‑fat if available

ingredients.name: Tandoor seasoning blend
ingredients.notes: mix smoked paprika, cumin, garlic powder, and a pinch of salt

instructions: Preheat a tandoor oven or a conventional oven to 425°F (220°C).
Butter a 9‑inch tart pan and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the low‑sodium cream, honey, and 1 tsp of sugar until smooth. Set aside.

In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add the potato slices in a single layer, cooking for 3–4 minutes per side until golden and slightly caramelized. Sprinkle with the tandoor seasoning, black pepper, and fresh dill, tossing to coat evenly. Remove from heat.
Arrange the caramelized potatoes in the prepared tart pan, pressing them gently to form an even layer.
Drizzle the cream mixture over the potatoes, ensuring an even distribution. Add a light dusting of lemon zest on top.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12–15 minutes until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are tender. Avoid overcooking to keep the crust crispy.
Remove from oven and let the tart rest for 5 minutes. Slice into wedges and serve warm. Optionally, drizzle a little extra lemon zest or a squeeze of fresh lemon juice before serving.
